Accuracy, macro-F1, weighted-F1, and per-class summaries do not always tell the same story. Macro-F1 remains valuable for class-risk detection, but Batch 24 narrows metric claims: metric_stress_validator is anti-hype support, not an automatic leakage detector or official benchmark validator.

Vehicle ambiguity is valuable because it prevents false clarity. Low-risk controls are valuable because they limit overgeneralization. Replay partials are valuable because they separate deterministic code replay from external-source stability. Metric-risk downgrades are valuable because they keep macro-F1 and accuracy claims bounded.
